---
title: "eda_covariates_sandbox"
output: html_notebook
---


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(scales)

dat <- readr::read_csv(
  here("data/processed/analysis/bats_viirs_covars_county_year_species.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

glimpse(dat)
```
```{r}
#add presence indicator
dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    is_present = as.integer(n_obs > 0)
  )

#Collapse to county x species summaries (covariates are on county scale)

county_species <- dat %>%
  group_by(GEOID, county_name, species_label) %>%
  summarise(
    presence_rate = mean(is_present, na.rm = TRUE),   # share of years present
    ever_present  = as.integer(any(is_present == 1)), # ever observed
    total_obs     = sum(n_obs, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_log_radiance = mean(log1p_radiance, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_developed = first(pct_developed),
    pct_protected = first(pct_protected),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
county_species
```



```{r}
#Distributions of pct_developed and pct_protected

county_only <- county_species %>% distinct(GEOID, county_name, pct_developed, pct_protected, mean_log_radiance)

ggplot(county_only, aes(pct_developed)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of pct_developed by county", x = "% developed", y = "Counties")

ggplot(county_only, aes(pct_protected)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of pct_protected by county", x = "% protected", y = "Counties")
```


```{r}
#Protection vs. Development

ggplot(county_only, aes(pct_developed, pct_protected)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Are protected counties less developed?",
    x = "% developed",
    y = "% protected"
  )

# Pearson correlation
cor_pro_dev_pearson <- cor(
  county_only$pct_protected,
  county_only$pct_developed,
  use = "complete.obs",
  method = "pearson"
)

# Spearman correlation
cor_pro_dev_spearman <- cor(
  county_only$pct_protected,
  county_only$pct_developed,
  use = "complete.obs",
  method = "spearman"
)

cor_pro_dev_pearson
cor_pro_dev_spearman
```

```{r}
#Log Radiance vs. Development

ggplot(county_only, aes(pct_developed, mean_log_radiance)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Is radiance mainly a proxy for development?",
    x = "% developed",
    y = "Mean log(1 + radiance)"
  )

# Pearson
cor_rad_dev_pearson <- cor(
  county_only$mean_log_radiance,
  county_only$pct_developed,
  use = "complete.obs",
  method = "pearson"
)

# Spearman
cor_rad_dev_spearman <- cor(
  county_only$mean_log_radiance,
  county_only$pct_developed,
  use = "complete.obs",
  method = "spearman"
)

cor_rad_dev_pearson
cor_rad_dev_spearman
```
Correlation is strongly positive for Log radiance and % developed, meaning the variables could be considered proxies for each other.

```{r}
#Presence rate vs. log radiance

ggplot(county_species, aes(mean_log_radiance, presence_rate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ species_label) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +
  labs(
    title = "Bat presence rate vs radiance (county × species)",
    x = "Mean log(1 + radiance)",
    y = "Presence rate (share of years with ≥1 obs)"
  )

```

```{r}
#Presence rate vs % developed

ggplot(county_species, aes(pct_developed, presence_rate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ species_label) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +
  labs(
    title = "Bat presence rate vs development",
    x = "% developed",
    y = "Presence rate"
  )
```
```{r}
#Presence rate vs % protected

ggplot(county_species, aes(pct_protected, presence_rate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ species_label) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +
  labs(
    title = "Bat presence rate vs protected land",
    x = "% protected (GAP 1–3)",
    y = "Presence rate"
  )
```
```{r}
#Dark and protected quadrant plot

#Make quartile bins to define quadrants
q_rad <- quantile(county_species$mean_log_radiance, 0.5, na.rm = TRUE)
q_pro <- quantile(county_species$pct_protected, 0.5, na.rm = TRUE)

county_species <- county_species %>%
  mutate(
    rad_bin = if_else(mean_log_radiance <= q_rad, "Darker half", "Brighter half"),
    prot_bin = if_else(pct_protected >= q_pro, "More protected", "Less protected")
  )

#Plot x radiance, y protected, color presence rate
ggplot(county_species, aes(mean_log_radiance, pct_protected, color = presence_rate)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = q_rad, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = q_pro, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ species_label) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(labels = percent) +
  labs(
    title = "Do dark + protected counties show higher bat presence?",
    x = "Mean log(1 + radiance)",
    y = "% protected land",
    color = "Presence rate"
  )
```
```{r}
#Observation bias check
#presence_rate rises with development? That may reflect access/observers

county_species %>%
  mutate(dev_quintile = ntile(pct_developed, 5)) %>%
  group_by(species_label, dev_quintile) %>%
  summarise(mean_presence = mean(presence_rate, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(factor(dev_quintile), mean_presence)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ species_label) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent) +
  labs(
    title = "Presence rate by development quintile",
    x = "Development quintile (1 = least developed, 5 = most developed)",
    y = "Mean presence rate"
  )

```
```{r}
#Presence rate by protection quintiles
county_species <- county_species %>%
  mutate(
    protection_quintile = ntile(pct_protected, 5)
  )

presence_by_protection <- county_species %>%
  group_by(species_label, protection_quintile) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_presence_rate = mean(presence_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_counties = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


ggplot(presence_by_protection,
       aes(protection_quintile, mean_presence_rate)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ species_label) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    title = "Bat presence rate by protection quintile",
    subtitle = "Each bar shows mean share of years with ≥1 observation\nCounties grouped by percent protected land",
    x = "Protection quintile (1 = least protected, 5 = most protected)",
    y = "Mean presence rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
